question_text: State the order of the eight planets in the Solar System, beginning with the planet closest to the Sun.
correct_answer: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune
explanation: The eight planets orbit the Sun in a fixed order based on their distance from it. The four inner planets (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars) are small and rocky, while the four outer planets (Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune) are large gas giants. Learning this order helps us understand the structure of our Solar System.
